_comment: Single source of truth for the Docker Scout 'clean bill of health' image set. Every image under `required` must earn a grade-A Docker Scout health score. CI enforces this in two places, both driven by this file (via the scout-config job): (1) PR time, .github/workflows/build.yml gates the policies that are deterministically checkable on a locally-built image -- fixable CRITICAL/HIGH CVEs and the default-non-root-user policy; (2) release time, .github/workflows/publish.yml runs `docker scout policy --exit-code` on the PUSHED image, which is the only place the true grade can be evaluated because supply-chain attestations (15 pts) only attach on push and base-image / high-profile-CVE / license policies are computed registry-side. `cve_net_extra` lists extra builder images that get the fixable CRITICAL/HIGH CVE net only, NOT the full grade-A bill of health. `exempt` lists images deliberately left OUT of the grade-A set with a documented reason -- each fails ONLY on third-party-bundled, upstream-frozen CVEs that buildenv cannot patch in place (no version lever exists here). A required image may still carry a documented OpenVEX `not_affected` statement under .github/vex/ for a specific upstream-frozen finding it has assessed as unreachable (applied via the cve-scan gate's vex-location) -- that is an auditable exploitability assessment, not an exemption, so the image stays grade-A and fully gated on everything else. Exempt images' upstream remediations (and any active VEX waivers + their drop triggers) are tracked in docs/upstream-cve-backlog.md. To require a new service, add it to `required` (and add the matching non-root USER + clean deps first). See README 'Image security policies'.

exempt.build-e2e: Assembly image: COPYs already-published third-party release artifacts (martin/shirotester/pdfserv Go binaries, newman's npm deps, wkhtmltopdf's patched-Qt .deb) carrying ~70 fixable CRITICAL/HIGH CVEs buildenv cannot patch without upstream rebuilds. Grade A is unreachable regardless of base hygiene; keeps its root default (a non-root flip earned no payoff). Upstream backlog: docs/upstream-cve-backlog.md.
exempt.build-js: azure-cli's vendored Python deps carry 2 fixable HIGH (pyjwt 2.12.0 -> 2.13.0 CVE-2026-48526; cryptography 46.0.7 -> 48.0.1 GHSA-537c-gmf6-5ccf). No buildenv lever: azure-cli 2.87.0 is already the latest release and still bundles the vulnerable versions; Microsoft must bump them. The big golang/stdlib cluster (was 4C + ~25H, from Debian's EOL-Go git-lfs) IS fixed here (official git-lfs binary). Stays non-root. Re-promote when a newer azure-cli ships patched deps. Upstream backlog: docs/upstream-cve-backlog.md.
exempt.build-swaggercodegen: All residual CVEs (1 CRITICAL + 16 HIGH) live inside the third-party swagger-codegen-cli-v3:3.0.34 JAR (snakeyaml, jackson-core, logback, commons-beanutils, commons-io, handlebars). buildenv only COPYs the jar; the fixed versions (e.g. commons-beanutils 1.11.0, handlebars 4.5.2) are too recent for the swagger-codegen 3.0.x line to have adopted, so even the latest 3.0.x jar would not fully clear them. Stays non-root. Re-promote if/when a swagger-codegen jar ships clean deps. Upstream backlog: docs/upstream-cve-backlog.md.
